name: dayjs
summary: 2KB immutable date time library alternative to Moment.js with the same modern API

story.problem: JavaScript's built-in Date object is notoriously broken — it's mutable (methods modify the object in place), months are zero-indexed, timezone handling is inconsistent, and there's no formatting built in. Moment.js solved this but grew to 67KB and became mutable. Day.js was created to offer the same ergonomics as moment.js in 2KB.
story.mental_model: Day.js objects are immutable — every operation returns a new instance, so you never accidentally mutate a date you're sharing. The API mirrors moment.js intentionally, making migration trivial: dayjs('2024-01-15').add(1, 'month').format('YYYY-MM-DD') reads exactly like human intent.
story.when_to_use: Use Day.js when you need date parsing, formatting, arithmetic (add/subtract days/months), or comparisons in browser code where bundle size matters. Its plugin system covers timezones, relative time ('3 hours ago'), duration, and ISO weeks.
story.when_not_to_use: For complex timezone-aware calendar applications, consider Temporal (the upcoming TC39 proposal) or date-fns with explicit timezone handling. Day.js's plugin system can grow large if you need many features.

alternatives.name: date-fns
alternatives.reason: Function-based instead of object-based; better tree-shaking since you import only the functions you use

alternatives.name: luxon
alternatives.reason: More robust timezone support built on the Intl API; larger bundle but better for international apps

alternatives.name: Temporal
alternatives.reason: Upcoming TC39 built-in that will make date libraries unnecessary — already available via polyfill

nodes.label: dayjs
nodes.summary: Default export is the factory: `dayjs()` returns an immutable Day.js object.

nodes.label: dayjs(input?)
nodes.summary: Parse a date: dayjs(), dayjs('2024-01-01'), dayjs(Date), dayjs(timestamp).

nodes.label: Dayjs (immutable)
nodes.summary: Immutable date object. All manipulation methods return a new Dayjs instance.

nodes.label: .format(template)
nodes.summary: Format a date: dayjs().format('YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss'). Tokens: YYYY, MM, DD, HH, mm, ss, ddd.

nodes.label: .diff()
nodes.summary: .diff(other, 'day') returns numeric difference in given unit.

nodes.label: Plugins (extend)
nodes.summary: dayjs.extend(plugin) adds functionality: relativeTime, utc, timezone, duration, isBetween, weekday.
